studyName: Serrated Colorectal Cancer: An Emerging Disease Subtype

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's sex, age, height, weight, marital status, colonoscopy/sigmoidoscopy history, history of polyps, bowel surgical history, Crohn's/colitis status, diabetes history, diabetes medication history, history of high cholesterol and associated medicines, history of aspirin and ibuprofen use, laxative use, vitamin use, and for females, pregnancy/gynecologic medical history, history of hormonal contraception use, history of hormone replacement therapy, dietary habits, physical activity, income, alcohol use, tobacco use, mental health status, birthplace, race, education, and weight history.

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MILD
variables.description: As an adult, have you participated regularly in any mild activity? Mild is defined as walking fast enough to cause your heart rate to increase somewhat. Regularly is defined as at least 30 minutes per week or longer, for at least 3 months in a row.

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MILDMOS
variables.description: How many months of the year did you participate in mild activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MILDNUMPERWEEK
variables.description: On average, how many hours per week did you participate in mild activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MILDNUMYRS
variables.description: How many years did you participate in mild activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MODEREATE
variables.description: As an adult, have you participated regularly in any moderate activity? Moderate is defined as any activity that caused your heart rate to increase somewhat, like gardening/yardwork or yoga. Regularly is defined as at least 30 minutes per week or longer, for at least 3 months in a row.

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MODEREATEMOS
variables.description: How many months of the year did you participate in moderate activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MODEREATENUMPERWEEK
variables.description: On average, how many hours per week did you participate in moderate activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_MODEREATENUMYRS
variables.description: How many years did you participate in moderate activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_SEDENTARY
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how many hours per day did you sit and watch TV or videos, use a computer, or read, outside of work or school?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_STRENUOUS
variables.description: As an adult, have you participated regularly in any strenuous activity? Strenuous is defined as any activity that increased your heart rate and caused you to sweat, such as jogging, swimming laps, bicycling, playing tennis, football, soccer, basketball, or aerobics. Regularly is defined as at least 30 minutes a week or longer, for at least 3 months in a row.

variables.name: ACTIVITY_STRENUOUSMOS
variables.description: How many months of the year did you participate in strenuous activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_STRENUOUSNUMPERWEEK
variables.description: On average, how many hours per week did you participate in strenuous activity?

variables.name: ACTIVITY_STRENUOUSNUMYRS
variables.description: How many years did you participate in strenuous activity?

variables.name: ACTUALSLEEP
variables.description: During the past month, about how many hours of actual sleep did you get in a typical 24-hour period?

variables.name: ADDCONAPPETITE
variables.description: I have a good appetite.

variables.name: ADDCONBODYAPPEAR
variables.description: I like the appearance of my body.

variables.name: ADDCONBOWELCTRL
variables.description: I have control of my bowels.

variables.name: ADDCONDIARRHEA
variables.description: I have diarrhea.

variables.name: ADDCONDIGESTION
variables.description: I can digest my food well.

variables.name: ADDCONLOSEWT
variables.description: I am losing weight.

variables.name: ADDCONOSTEMBARRASS
variables.description: I am embarassed by my ostomy appliance.

variables.name: ADDCONOSTEMCARE
variables.description: Caring for my ostomy appliance is difficult.

variables.name: ADDCONOSTOMY
variables.description: Do you have an ostomy appliance?

variables.name: ADDCONSTOMACH
variables.description: I have swelling or cramps in my stomach area.

variables.name: AE_1ST_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) How old were you when you first took this medication?

variables.name: AE_FRQ
variables.description: (For females only) In total, how many months or years did you take tamoxifen, raloxifene, or other anti-estrogen medications?

variables.name: AE_INT
variables.description: (For females only) Interval of frequency tamoxifen, raloxifene, or other anti-estrogen medications taken.

variables.name: AE_TWO_YEARSAGO
variables.description: (For females only) Were you taking this medication about 2 years ago?

variables.name: AFRAID
variables.description: People act as if they are afraid of you.

variables.name: AGE_EPI
variables.description: What is your age?

variables.name: ANTI_ESTROGEN
variables.description: (For females only) Have you ever taken tamoxifen, raloxifen, or other anti-estrogen medication, for 6 months or longer?

variables.name: ASPIRIN
variables.description: Have you ever taken aspirin (such as Anacin, Bufferin, Bayer, Excedrin, and Ecotrin) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_CURRENT
variables.description: Are you currently taking aspirin?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take it?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_INT
variables.description: Interval in which aspirin was taken.

variables.name: ASPIRIN_LEN
variables.description: In total, how many months or years did you take aspirin?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_REG
variables.description: About two years ago, were you taking aspirin regularly?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time aspirin was taken.

variables.name: CHOLES_MED
variables.description: Did you ever take medication to control your high cholesterol or triglyercides?

variables.name: CIG
variables.description: Have you ever smoked at least one cigarette a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: CIG_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you first started smoking at least one cigarette a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: CIG_2YRS
variables.description: About 2 years ago were you still smoking at least one cigarette a day?

variables.name: CIG_CURR
variables.description: Do you still smoke at least one cigarette a day?

variables.name: CIG_PD
variables.description: During periods when you smoked regularly, how many cigarettes did you typically smoke in a day? (1 pack = 20 cigarettes.)

variables.name: CIG_STOP_AGE
variables.description: When did you permanently stop smoking at least 1 cigarette a day?

variables.name: CIG_YRS
variables.description: How many years in total did you smoke at least one cigarette per day for 3 months or longer? (If you stopped and restarted at least once, count only the time when you were smoking.)

variables.name: COLITIS
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had ulcerative colitis?

variables.name: COLITIS_1ST_AGE
variables.description: Hold old were you when your doctor first told you that you had ulcerative colitis?

variables.name: COLON_REM
variables.description: Have you ever had any of your large bowel or colon removed?

variables.name: COLSCOPE
variables.description: Have you ever had a colonoscopy?

variables.name: COLSCOPE_1ST_AGE
variables.description: When did you first have a colonoscopy?

variables.name: COLSCOPE_LST_AGE
variables.description: When did you last have a colonoscopy?

variables.name: COLSCOPE_NO
variables.description: How many separate colonoscopies have you had?

variables.name: COL_REM_1ST_AGE
variables.description: When did you first have any of your bowel or colon removed?

variables.name: COL_REM_EXT
variables.description: Was your large bowel or colon completely removed or was only part of it removed?

variables.name: COL_REM_LST_AGE
variables.description: When did you last have this operation to remove all or part of your bowel or colon?

variables.name: COL_SURG
variables.description: Have you had more than one surgery to remove your bowel or colon?

variables.name: CROHNS
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had Crohn's disease?

variables.name: CROHNS_1ST_AGE
variables.description: When did your doctor first tell you that you had Crohn's disease?

variables.name: Calcium
variables.description: Have you ever taken calcium pills or tablets or calcium based antacids at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: Calcium_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take calcium pills or tablets?

variables.name: Calcium_Len
variables.description: In total, how many months or years have you taken calcium pills or tablets?

variables.name: Calcium_Reg
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking calcium pills or tablets regularly?

variables.name: Calcium_int
variables.description: Interval for frequency calcium pills or tablets taken.

variables.name: Calcium_time
variables.description: Are you currently taking calcium pills or tablets or calcium based antacids?

variables.name: DIABETES
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had diabetes, also known as diabetes mellitus? (Note: this does not include gestational diabetes/diabetes you had only during pregnancy)

variables.name: DIAB_AGE
variables.description: When did your doctor first tell you that you had diabetes.

variables.name: DIAB_MEDS
variables.description: Did you ever take medication to control your diabetes?

variables.name: DISCRIM_AGE
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Age

variables.name: DISCRIM_ANCESTRY
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences?  Ancestry or national origins.

variables.name: DISCRIM_EDU
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Education or income

variables.name: DISCRIM_GENDER
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Gender

variables.name: DISCRIM_HEIGHT
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Height

variables.name: DISCRIM_OTHER
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Other. There were a variety of reasons that were included in this response and it included other reasons outside of discriminatory ones.

variables.name: DISCRIM_PHYSICAL
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Physical disability

variables.name: DISCRIM_RACE
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Race

variables.name: DISCRIM_RELIGION
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Religion

variables.name: DISCRIM_SEXUAL
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Sexual orientation

variables.name: DISCRIM_WEIGHT
variables.description: What do you think is the reason for these experiences? Weight

variables.name: DRNKALCOHOLBEV
variables.description: [Please answer based on the last ten years.]  During that time, did you ever drink any alcoholic beverages at least once a week for 6 months or longer? Alcoholic beverages include beer, hard cider, wine, sake, liquor, spirits, mixed drinks, cocktails, wine coolers, and hard lemonade.

variables.name: DRNKHOWLONG
variables.description: For how many months or years did you drink at least one alcoholic beverage per week during that time frame?

variables.name: DRNKHOWLONG_INT
variables.description: Number of months or number of years you drank at least one alcoholic beverage per week during that time frame

variables.name: DRNKHOWMUCH_BEER
variables.description: How many drinks did you typically drink of 12 oz. can/bottles of beer or cider?

variables.name: DRNKHOWMUCH_BEER_INT
variables.description: Number of months or number of years you typically drank 12 oz. can/bottles of beer or cider

variables.name: DRNKHOWMUCH_LIQUOR
variables.description: How many drinks did you typically drink of 1.5 oz. shots of liquor?

variables.name: DRNKHOWMUCH_LIQUOR_INT
variables.description: Number of months or number of years you typically drank 1.5 oz. shots of liquor

variables.name: DRNKHOWMUCH_WINE
variables.description: How many drinks did you typically drink of 4-6 oz. glasses of wine?

variables.name: DRNKHOWMUCH_WINE_INT
variables.description: Number of months or number of years you typically drank 4-6 oz. glasses of wine

variables.name: D_COMBINATION_PILLS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your diabetes? Combination pills

variables.name: D_INSULIN
variables.description: Insulin (injections, pump or nasal spray)

variables.name: D_METFORMIN
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your diabetes?  Metformin pills.

variables.name: D_OTHER_PILLS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your diabetes? Other pills

variables.name: D_SULPHONVLUREA_PILLS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your diabetes? Sulphonylurea pills

variables.name: D_THIAZOLIDINEDIONE_PILLS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your diabetes? Thiazolidinedione pills

variables.name: EDUCATION
variables.description: What is the highest level of education that you have completed?

variables.name: ESTROGEN
variables.description: (For females only) What kind of hormone replacement therapy medication was it? Estrogen therapy.

variables.name: EWBCONDITIONWORSE
variables.description: I worry that my condition will get worse.

variables.name: EWBCOPING
variables.description: I am satisfied with how I am coping with my illness.

variables.name: EWBDYINGWORRY
variables.description: I worry about dying.

variables.name: EWBFEELNERVOUS
variables.description: I feel nervous.

variables.name: EWBFEELSAD
variables.description: I feel sad.

variables.name: EWBLOSEHOPE
variables.description: I am losing hope in the fight against my illness.

variables.name: FRUIT
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how many servings of fruit did you eat? A serving of fruit is: 1 medium fresh fruit; 6 oz of fruit juice; one half cup of chopped, cooked or canned fruit; one quarter cup of dried fruit

variables.name: FRUIT_INT
variables.description: Interval of frequency fruit.

variables.name: FWBCONTENTQOL
variables.description: I am content with the quality of my life right now.

variables.name: FWBENJOYLIFE
variables.description: I am able to enjoy life.

variables.name: FWBILLACCEPT
variables.description: I have accepted my illness.

variables.name: FWBSENJOYFUN
variables.description: I am enjoying the things I usually do for fun.

variables.name: FWBSLEEPWELL
variables.description: I am sleeping well.

variables.name: FWBWORK
variables.description: I am able to work (include work at home).

variables.name: FWBWORKFULFILLING
variables.description: My work (include work at home) is fulfilling.

variables.name: GB_REM_AGE
variables.description: When did you have your gallbladder removed?

variables.name: GB_SURG
variables.description: Have you had your gallbladder removed?

variables.name: HARASSED
variables.description: You are threatened or harassed.

variables.name: HC_1ST_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) How old were you when you first used hormonal contraceptives?

variables.name: HC_CONT
variables.description: (For females only) Were you still using them about two years ago?

variables.name: HC_MED_BILE
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Bile-acid sequestrants

variables.name: HC_MED_FIBRATES
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Fibrates

variables.name: HC_MED_INHIBITORS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Cholesterol absorbtion inhibitors

variables.name: HC_MED_INJECTABLE
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Injectable medications

variables.name: HC_MED_NIACIN
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Niacin

variables.name: HC_MED_OTHER
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Other

variables.name: HC_MED_STATINS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Statins

variables.name: HC_MED_VITAMINS
variables.description: What type of medication did you use to control your high cholesterol or triglyerides? Vitamins/supplements

variables.name: HC_YRS
variables.description: In total, how many years did you take hormonal contraceptives?

variables.name: HEIGHT
variables.description: About how tall are you, without your shoes on (in centimeters)? (1 inch = 2.54 centimeters)

variables.name: HEMOCCULT
variables.description: Have you ever had a test for blood in your stool, sometimes called a fecal occult blood test (FOBT) or fecal immunochemical test (FIT)?

variables.name: HEMOCCULT_1ST_AGE
variables.description: When did you first have a fecal occult blood test (FOBT) or fecal immunochemical test (FIT)?

variables.name: HEMOCCULT_LST_AGE
variables.description: When did you last have a fecal occult blood test (FOBT) or fecal immunochemical test (FIT)?

variables.name: HEMOCCULT_NO
variables.description: How many separate tests have you had?

variables.name: HORM_CONTRACEP
variables.description: (For females only) Have you ever used birth control pills or other hormonal contraceptives for one year or longer?

variables.name: HRT
variables.description: Have you ever used a pill, patch, or implant form of hormone replacement therapy for 6 months or longer? (For females only) Do not include hormone therapy that was prescribed for birth control;  delivered by injections, vaginal creams, or vaginal suppositories.

variables.name: HRT_1ST_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) How old were you when you first took hormone replacement therapy medication?

variables.name: HRT_FRQ
variables.description: (For females only) In total, how many months or years did you take hormone replacement therapy medication?

variables.name: HRT_INT
variables.description: (For females only) Interval for frequency of estrogen-only medication taken.

variables.name: HRT_TWO_YEARSAGO
variables.description: (For females only) Were you still using hormone replacement therapy medication about 2 years ago?

variables.name: HYST_B_OV
variables.description: (For females only) What type of gynecologic surgery did you have? Hysterectomy along with both ovaries.

variables.name: HYST_B_OV_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) When did you first have this surgery?

variables.name: HYST_ONLY
variables.description: (For females only) Have you ever had a gynecologic surgery?  Do not include c-section, cyst removals or tubal ligation.  What type of surgery was it?  Hysterectomy only.

variables.name: HYST_ONLY_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) When did you first have this surgery?

variables.name: HYST_P_OV
variables.description: (For females only) What type of gynecologic surgery did you have? Hysterectomy along with one ovary or partial ovary?

variables.name: HYST_P_OV_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) When did you first have this surgery?

variables.name: H_CHOLES
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had high cholesterol or high triglycerides?

variables.name: H_CHOLES_AGE
variables.description: When did your doctor first tell you that you had high cholesterol or triglycerides?

variables.name: IBUPROFEN
variables.description: Not including aspirin, have you ever taken other non-steroidal anti-inflammatory medication (NSAID), such as ibuprofen, Advil, Aleve, Naprosyn, Motrin, Celebrex, Tramadol, Naproxen, or Meloxicam, at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: IB_CURRENT
variables.description: Are you currently taking an NSAIDs-type of medication?

variables.name: IB_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take ibuprofen-based medications?

variables.name: IB_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency in which ibuprofen-based medications were taken.

variables.name: IB_LEN
variables.description: In total, how many months or years did you take an NSAIDs-type medication?

variables.name: IB_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking ibuprofen-based medications regularly?

variables.name: IB_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time ibuprofen-based medication was taken.

variables.name: INCOME
variables.description: As of about two years ago, which of the following best describes your total annual household income from all sources before taxes?

variables.name: INCOME_CURRENT
variables.description: Which of the following best describes your current total annual household income from all sources before taxes?

variables.name: LB_1ST_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) How old were you at the first live birth?

variables.name: LB_LST_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) How old were you at the last live birth?

variables.name: LESSRESPECT
variables.description: In your day-to-day life, how often have any of the following things happened to you?  You were treated with less courtesy or respect than other people.

variables.name: LIVE_BIR_NO
variables.description: (For females only) How many of your pregnancies resulted in live births?

variables.name: MARSTAT
variables.description: Are you currently married or living as married?

variables.name: MPS_GS
variables.description: (For females only) Why did your menstrual periods stop permanently? Gynecologic surgery.

variables.name: MPS_NM
variables.description: (For females only) Why did your menstrual periods stop permanently? Natural menopause.

variables.name: MPS_OTH
variables.description: (For females only) Why did your menstrual periods stop permanently? Other reasons.

variables.name: MPS_RC
variables.description: (For females only) Why did your menstrual periods stop permanently? Radiation or chemotherapy.

variables.name: MP_HRT
variables.description: For females only Were you still having menstrual periods when you first took these hormones?

variables.name: MP_STOP
variables.description: (For females only) Have your menstrual periods stopped permanently, that is for 6 months or more, not due to contraceptives?

variables.name: MP_STOP_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) How old were you when your periods stopped permanently?

variables.name: MULTIVITAMIN
variables.description: Have you ever taken multivitamin pills or tablets (not individual vitamins) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: MV_CURRENT
variables.description: Are you currently taking multivitamins?

variables.name: MV_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take multivitamin pills or tablets?

variables.name: MV_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency in which multivitamin pills or tablets were taken.

variables.name: MV_LEN
variables.description: In total, how many months or years did you take multivitamin pills or tablets?

variables.name: MV_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking multivitamin pills or tablets regularly?

variables.name: MV_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time multivitamin pills or tablets was taken.

variables.name: NOTSMART
variables.description: People act as if they think you are not smart.

variables.name: OL_CURRENT
variables.description: Are you currently taking laxatives?

variables.name: OL_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take laxitives?

variables.name: OL_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency laxatives were taken.

variables.name: OL_LEN
variables.description: In total, how many months or years did you take laxatives?

variables.name: OL_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking laxitives regularly?

variables.name: OL_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time laxatives were taken.

variables.name: OL_TYPE
variables.description: What type of laxative did you take?

variables.name: OTH_LAX
variables.description: Have you ever taken laxatives at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: OV_B_REM
variables.description: (for females only) What type of gynecologic surgery did you have? Both ovaries removed without hysterectomy.

variables.name: OV_B_REM_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) When did you first have this surgery?

variables.name: OV_REM
variables.description: (For females only) What type of gynecologic surgery did you have? One ovary removed in whole or part without hysterectomy.

variables.name: OV_REM_AGE
variables.description: (For females only) When did you first have this surgery?

variables.name: POLYPS
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had polyps in your large bowel or colon or rectum?

variables.name: POLYP_1ST_AGE
variables.description: When did your doctor first tell you that you had polyps?

variables.name: POLYP_ADEN
variables.description: Adenomatous (pre-cancerous) polyps

variables.name: POLYP_HYPER
variables.description: Hyperplastic polyps

variables.name: POLYP_LST_AGE
variables.description: When did your doctor last tell you that you had polyps?

variables.name: POLYP_OTH
variables.description: Other polyps

variables.name: POLYP_SERRATED
variables.description: Serrated polyps

variables.name: POLYP_TOLD
variables.description: Have you been told by a doctor that you had polyps more than once?

variables.name: POORSERVICE
variables.description: You receive poorer service than other people at restaurants or stores.

variables.name: PREGNANT
variables.description: (For females only) Have you ever been pregnant?

variables.name: PREG_NO
variables.description: (For females only) How many times have you been pregnant? Please include miscarriages, stillbirths, tubal pregnancies, and abortions. If you are currently pregnant, do not include current pregnancy.

variables.name: PROCMEATSERVNUM
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how often did you eat processed meat, such as bacon, lunch meat, or hot dogs? This does not include processed poultry meat such as turkey or chicken deli meat. Please include any packaged meals that contain processed meat. This can overlap with servings of red meat. (This is for the number of servings)

variables.name: PROCMEATSERV_INT
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how often did you eat processed meat, such as bacon, lunch meat, or hot dogs? This does not include processed poultry meat such as turkey or chicken deli meat. Please include any packaged meals that contain processed meat. This can overlap with servings of red meat.

variables.name: PROGEST
variables.description: (For females only) What kind of hormone replacement therapy medication was it? Progesterone or progestin therapy

variables.name: PSSANGERED
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you been angered because of things that were outside of your control?

variables.name: PSSCONFIDENT
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you felt confident about your ability to handle your personal problems?

variables.name: PSSCOPE
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you found that you could not cope with all the things you had to do?

variables.name: PSSIRRITATIONS
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you been able to control irritations in your life?

variables.name: PSSNERVOUS
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you felt nervous and "stressed"?

variables.name: PSSNOCONTROL
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you felt that you were unable to control the important things in your life?

variables.name: PSSONTOP
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you felt that you were on top of things?

variables.name: PSSOVERCOME
variables.description: In the last month, how often habe you felt difficulties were piling up so high that you could not overcome them?

variables.name: PSSUPSET
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you been upset because of something that happened unexpectedly?

variables.name: PSSYOURWAY
variables.description: In the last month, how often have you felt that things were going your way?

variables.name: PWBFAMILYNEEDS
variables.description: Because of my physical condition, I have trouble meeting the needs of my family.

variables.name: PWBFORCEDBEDREST
variables.description: I am forced to spend time in bed.

variables.name: PWBILL
variables.description: I feel ill.

variables.name: PWBLACKENERGY
variables.description: I have a lack of energy.

variables.name: PWBNAUSEA
variables.description: I have nausea.

variables.name: PWBPAIN
variables.description: I have pain.

variables.name: PWBTREATMNTEFFECTS
variables.description: I am bothered by side effects of treatment.

variables.name: RED_MEAT
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how many servings of red meat (not chicken or fish) did you eat? A serving of red meat is: 2-3 ounces of red meat;A piece of meat about the size of a deck of cards. Red meats include beef, veal, lamb, pork, venison, or mutton in any form (steak, hamburger, prime rib, ribs, bacon, sausage, hot dogs, lunch meat). Please include any packaged meals that contain a serving of red meat.

variables.name: RED_MEAT_INT
variables.description: Interval of frequency of red meat.

variables.name: RM_COOKED_FRQ
variables.description: On average, how many of those servings of red meat were cooked by the following methods: pan-frying, broiling, grilling, or barbecuing? This does not include baking or boiling.

variables.name: RM_COOKED_INT
variables.description: Intervals of frequency of red meat ate that was cooked by pan-frying, broiling, grilling, or barbecuing.

variables.name: RM_IN
variables.description: On average, when you ate red meat cooked by these methods which of the following best describes its inside appearance (how well it was done)?

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: What is your gender?

variables.name: SIGSCOPE
variables.description: Have you ever had a sigmoidoscopy?

variables.name: SIGSCOPE_1ST_AGE
variables.description: When did you first have a sigmoidoscopy?

variables.name: SIGSCOPE_LST_AGE
variables.description: When did you last have a sigmoidoscopy?

variables.name: SIGSCOPE_NO
variables.description: How many separate sigmoidoscopies have you had?

variables.name: SLEEPQUALITY
variables.description: During the past month, how would you rate your sleep quality overall?

variables.name: SMOKE_CIGAR
variables.description: Have you ever used cigars once a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: SMOKE_ECIG
variables.description: Have you ever used E-cigarettes, E-hookah, or vape pens once a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: SMOKE_OTHER
variables.description: Have you ever used other forms of smoking once a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: SMOKE_OTH_EVER
variables.description: Have you ever used other tobacco products once a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: SMOKE_PIPES
variables.description: Have you ever used pipes once a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: SMOKE_SMOKELESS
variables.description: Have you ever used smokeless tobacco (e.g. sniff, chew, dip) once a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: SPICYFOODSERVNUM
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how often did you eat spicy foods (pepper, curry, ginger, cinnamon, turmeric, or hot sauce)? (This is for the number of servings)

variables.name: SPICYFOODSERVNUM_INT
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how often did you eat spicy foods (pepper, curry, ginger, cinnamon, turmeric, or hot sauce)?

variables.name: SWBCLOSETOFRIENDS
variables.description: I feel close to my friends.

variables.name: SWBCLOSETOPARTNER
variables.description: I feel close to my partner (or the person who is my main support).

variables.name: SWBFAMILYACCEPTILL
variables.description: My family has accepted my illness.

variables.name: SWBFAMILYCOMMILL
variables.description: I am satisfied with family communication about my illness.

variables.name: SWBFAMILYSUPPORT
variables.description: I get emotional support from my family.

variables.name: SWBFRIENDSUPPORT
variables.description: I get support from my friends.

variables.name: SWBSEXLIFE
variables.description: I am satisfied with my sex life.

variables.name: S_RACE1
variables.description: What is your race?

variables.name: S_RACE2
variables.description: What is your race?

variables.name: VEG
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how many servings of vegetables did you eat? A serving of vegetables is: 1 cup raw leafy vegetables; one half cup of other vegetables, cooked or chopped raw; 6 ounces of vegetable juice

variables.name: VEG_INT
variables.description: Interval of frequency of vegetables.

variables.name: WEIGHT_2YRS
variables.description: How much did you weigh about 2 years ago (in kilograms)? (1 pound = 0.453 kilogram)

variables.name: WEIGHT_CURRENT
variables.description: What is your current weight (in kilograms)? (1 pound = 0.453 kilogram)

variables.name: birthpl_us
variables.description: In what city, state, and country were you born?

variables.name: study_ID
variables.description: Double de-identified number that uniquely identifies an individual.

variables.name: weight_2yrs_lbs
variables.description: How much did you weigh about 2 years ago (in pounds)?

variables.name: weight_current_lbs
variables.description: What is your current weight (in pounds)?

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's tumor number, age at diagnosis, sex, case-control status, individual tumor locations, individual tumor histological types, individual tumor behaviors, source of diagnosis information, SEER or self reported race values, and hispanic ethnicity.

variables.name: AgeDx
variables.description: Age at diagnosis.

variables.name: Behav
variables.description: Fifth digit of the ICD-O-3 morphology code which designates the behaviour of this tumor. Coding for this field is based on SEER, NAACCR and AcoS guidelines.

variables.name: ETHNIC_BL
variables.description: Response to question "Do you consider yourself to be Hispanic/Latino? Yes, No, Don't know/Refused"

variables.name: Histo
variables.description: First four digits of the ICD-O-3 morphology code which designates the histologic type of this tumor. Coding for this field is based on SEER, NAACCR and AcoS guidelines.

variables.name: RACE1
variables.description: What is your race? (Codes are from EER/NAACCR/ACOS standards) This is a multiple choice question where participants were allowed to choose any that apply.

variables.name: RACE2
variables.description: What is your race? (Codes are from EER/NAACCR/ACOS standards) This is a multiple choice question where participants were allowed to choose any that apply.

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Are you male or female?

variables.name: Site
variables.description: Location where this tumor originated in as much detail as is known. An ICD-O-3 code is provided.

variables.name: TumorNo
variables.description: Starting with "1", assigned to each tumor for a given individual in the order of diagnosis.

variables.name: case_status
variables.description: Case (CRC-only) or control status

variables.name: dxsource
variables.description: Source of diagnosis information.

variables.name: study_ID
variables.description: Person identifier uniquely identifying an individual.

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's tumor number, age at diagnosis, sex, individual tumor locations, individual tumor histological types, individual tumor behaviors, source of diagnosis information, SEER or self reported race values, and hispanic ethnicity.

variables.name: AgeDx
variables.description: Age at diagnosis.

variables.name: Behav
variables.description: Fifth digit of the ICD-O-3 morphology code which designates the behaviour of this tumor. Coding for this field is based on SEER, NAACCR and AcoS guidelines.

variables.name: Histo
variables.description: First four digits of the ICD-O-3 morphology code which designates the histologic type of this tumor. Coding for this field is based on SEER, NAACCR and AcoS guidelines.

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Code for the biological sex of the patient.

variables.name: Site
variables.description: Location where this tumor originated in as much detail as is known. An ICD-O-3 code is provided.

variables.name: TumorNo
variables.description: Starting with "1", assigned to each tumor for a given individual in the order of diagnosis.

variables.name: dxsource
variables.description: Source of diagnosis information.

variables.name: hispanic
variables.description: Code identifying persons of Spanish or Hispanic origin. This code is used by hospital and central registries to show the "best guess" as to whether or not the person should be classified as Hispanic for purposes of calculating cancer rates. If the patient has multiple tumors, all records should have the same code.Reference to Census 2000 definitions for ethnicity and race: http://www.census.gov/prod/cen2000/doc/sf2.pdf. All information resources should be used to determine the correct code, including: - Stated ethnicity in the medical record - Stated Hispanic origin on the death certificate - Birthplace - Information about life history and/or language spoken found during the abstracting process - Patient's last name [2230] or maiden name

variables.name: race1
variables.description: Code the patient's race. Race is coded separately from Spanish/Hispanic Origin. All tumors for the same patient should have the same race codes. If the patient is multiracial, code other race using RACE 2. For coding instructions and race code history see the current SEER Program Coding and Staging Manual.Reference to Census 2000 definitions for ethnicity and race: http://www.census.gov/prod/cen2000/doc/sf2.pdf (Appendix G).

variables.name: race2
variables.description: Code the patient's race. Race is coded separately from Spanish/Hispanic Origin. All tumors for the same patient should have the same race codes. If the patient is multiracial, code all races using RACE 2 through RACE 5. For coding instructions and race code history see the current SEER Program Coding and Staging Manual3.Reference to Census 2000 definitions for ethnicity and race: http://www.census.gov/prod/cen2000/doc/sf2.pdf (Appendix G).

variables.name: study_ID
variables.description: Identifier uniquely identifying an individual.

tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, tumor status, and tumor number in the order of diagnosis.

variables.name: TumorNo
variables.description: Starting with "1", assigned to each tumor for a given individual in the order of diagnosis

variables.name: analyte_type
variables.description: Analyte type

variables.name: body_site
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: is_tumor
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: sample_ID
variables.description: Sample identifier.

tables.description: This subject consent data table reports each subject's subject ID, consent, and biological sex.

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Code for the sex of the patient.

variables.name: consent
variables.description: Consent group

variables.name: study_ID
variables.description: Double deidentified number that uniquely identifies an individual.

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: sample_ID
variables.description: Sample identifier.

variables.name: study_ID
variables.description: Identifier uniquely identifying an individual.
